sources.notes: Used to read the range-update statement and constraints.
Used to confirm the decomposition of lowbit into power-of-two divisibility layers and the prefix-sum implementation.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: For many range watering operations [l,r], add f(i-l+1) = (i-l+1) * lowbit(i-l+1) to every covered position and output final values.
annotation.constraints: Sum of n and q is 2e5, so each range update must be processed in logarithmic or amortized logarithmic time rather than iterating the full interval.
annotation.core_idea: Use lowbit(x) = 1 + sum over powers 2^b for which 2^(b+1) divides x. For each bit layer, positions satisfying the divisibility condition form an arithmetic progression relative to l. Maintain per-layer difference arrays for the constant and linear coefficients so that x * lowbit(x) is accumulated as position-dependent range additions, then prefix-sum all layers into the answer.
annotation.complexity: O((n + q) log n) per test file total.
tricks: separate linear coefficient i and offset l-1

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: For every query and every relevant power of two, update difference arrays on positions congruent to l-1 modulo that power, then prefix-sum coefficients to form final water amounts.

tags.evidence: The function being updated is x multiplied by its least significant set bit, and the solution decomposes that lowbit by powers of two.
tags.description: Uses the least significant set bit or divisibility by powers of two as a main decomposition.
tags.created_reason: Lowbit appears often enough in Fenwick-tree-adjacent and power-of-two decomposition problems to be directly searchable.

tags.evidence: Range updates are stored in per-layer difference arrays and materialized by prefix sums.
tags.description: Range updates are accumulated with difference arrays and prefix sums.
tags.created_reason: Difference arrays are a common range-update technique worth querying independently from generic prefix sums.

tags.evidence: The lowbit decomposition relies on the condition that powers of two divide i-l+1.
After all difference updates, prefix sums recover each layer's contribution.
The final value is assembled by independently maintaining contributions of each power-of-two layer.
